channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Avante anuncia pré-candidatura do escritor Augusto Cury à Presidência da República
item.description: The Avante party announced the pre-candidacy of writer Augusto Cury for President of Brazil in the 2026 elections, emphasizing emotional balance, education, and humanized governance. Cury, a physician and psychologist, is a best-selling author who states his candidacy is a collective journey, not a pursuit of power. He joins other pre-candidates including Lula, Caiado, Flávio Bolsonaro, Zema, Renan Santos, and Aldo Rebelo.

item.title: A turbulenta relação entre dois ministros importantes de Lula no governo
item.description: The relationship between Defense Minister José Múcio and Chief of Staff Rui Costa deteriorated after Costa overlooked the Defense Ministry's achievements in a report presented to Lula. Múcio reacted sharply, claiming his ministry had more accomplishments than the Ministry of Women, during a ministerial meeting. Despite the tension, Múcio remains in government due to his personal commitment to Lula, as the country faces growing global armed conflicts requiring a strong military agenda.

item.title: Janja celebra a Páscoa com cachorras fantasiadas de coelhinhas
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva shared an AI-generated photo of her three dogs dressed as bunny girls to celebrate Easter, wishing love, smiles, and unity. She also appeared in a post by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, who emphasized renewal and family peace. The post was made on Sunday, April 5, 2026, on Instagram.

item.title: Sem propostas por ora, Flávio Bolsonaro e Ronaldo Caiado se apoiam em anistia aos golpistas
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro and Ronaldo Caiado, 2026 presidential pre-candidates, converge on proposing amnesty for the January 8 rioters, including Jair Bolsonaro, as a strategy to capture Bolsonarist voters. Both adopt a frontal opposition stance to Lula’s government, with Caiado emphasizing his executive experience and Flávio seeking to preserve Bolsonaro’s legacy, while competing for influence in agribusiness and evangelical electorates.

item.title: Ala do PT contesta filiação de Kátia Abreu
item.description: A minority faction of the PT is challenging the affiliation of Kátia Abreu, arguing that the process violated internal procedures by bypassing state directory deliberation and criticizing her agribusiness background as incompatible with the party's principles. Despite the challenge, the national leadership is expected to uphold her membership, as Abreu states she intends to support Lulu in defending democracy and his reelection bid.

item.title: MPE recorre de decisão do STF que liberou candidatura de Garotinho no RJ
item.description: The Electoral Public Ministry (MPE) appealed the Supreme Federal Court's (STF) decision that suspended the electoral effects of former Rio de Janeiro governor Anthony Garotinho's conviction, thereby allowing his candidacy. The MPE contends that the STF ruling violates the Clean Record Law and seeks to overturn the benefit granted to the politician.

item.title: Páscoa é momento de renovação e união, diz Lula em mensagem
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva released an Easter message emphasizing renewal and family unity, posted alongside First Lady Janja and children. Vice President Geraldo Alckmin also commented, framing Easter as a celebration of God's love and an opportunity to strengthen familial bonds. Both messages were shared on social media this Sunday (April 5).

item.title: STF condena a 14 anos de prisão empresário catarinense que doou R$ 500 para ônibus fretado para 8 de janeiro
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court (STF) sentenced Santa Catarina businessman Alcides Hahn to 14 years in prison for transferring R$500 to fund a chartered bus that transported demonstrators from Blumenau to Brasília on January 8, 2023. Hahn was convicted of violent abolition of the democratic rule of law, coup d’état, qualified damage, deterioration of public heritage, and criminal association. His defense filed an appeal, which was removed from the docket on March 20.

item.title: A forma que Geraldo Alckmin encontrou de tentar ajudar Fernando Haddad na eleição
item.description: Geraldo Alckmin, now Lula's running mate, is leveraging his position to boost Fernando Haddad's campaign in São Paulo. He has been calling mayors in the state's interior, promising the release of parliamentary amendments in exchange for support for the petista candidate. Party leaders view the effort skeptically, noting that while the funds are available, the political credit will go to Lula, not Haddad.

item.title: Eduardo Bolsonaro ataca Nikolas Ferreira e diz que 'fama fez mal' ao deputado
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro publicly criticized Nikolas Ferreira on X, stating that 'fame did him harm'. The exchange reflects growing tensions between different factions of Brazil's far-right. The episode highlights internal divisions within Bolsonarism ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Wilson Lima renuncia como governador do Amazonas e cita prazo de desincompatibilização
item.description: The governor of Amazonas, Wilson Lima (União Brasil), resigned from office on the night of April 4 through a letter published in the Official Gazette of the State Legislative Assembly (ALEAM). He stated the decision is irrevocable and aimed at fulfilling the electoral law's disassociation requirement, likely in preparation for a potential candidacy in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Políticos publicam mensagens de Páscoa nas redes sociais
item.description: Brazilian politicians, including President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, First Lady Janja Lula da Silva, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, former governor Romeu Zema, and others, posted Easter messages on social media on Sunday, April 5, 2026. The posts emphasized themes of renewal, family unity, Christian faith, solidarity, and hope, reflecting the ideological leanings of each figure.

item.title: A 6 meses das eleições, Janja prioriza pautas relacionadas a mulheres
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva has prioritized women's issues in her agenda, with 31 of 124 disclosed commitments between October 2025 and April 2026 related to gender equality, reflecting the government's electoral strategy. Her work includes leading the National Pact to Combat Femicide and influencing Lula’s stronger stance on gender-based violence following public emotional displays. She also focuses on environmental protection (22 commitments) and international engagements (19), including a trip to Asia for the Brazil-Korea Business Forum.

item.title: Janja soma 170 dias fora do Brasil desde 2023
item.description: First Lady Janja Lula da Silva has spent 170 days abroad since 2023, including 13 days in 2026 across three international trips to South Korea, the UAE, and the United States. Her March 8–13 visit to New York included participation in the UN’s 70th Commission on the Status of Women, focusing on gender and social policies, reinforcing her role as a key advocate in these areas. With women comprising 53% of the Brazilian electorate ahead of the 2026 elections, her agenda has prioritized initiatives like the National Pact to Combat Femicide.

item.title: Michelle é uma unha encravada nos pés de barro dos enteados
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro is portrayed as a strict stepmother who delayed her involvement in Flávio Bolsonaro’s pre-campaign and is now dismantling narratives crafted by Carlos and Eduardo Bolsonaro that frame jailed former president Jair Bolsonaro as a political victim.

item.title: Botafogo provoca Vasco após vitória no clássico pelo Brasileirão; veja
item.description: Botafogo defeated Vasco 2-1 at São Januário in the 10th round of the 2026 Brasileirão, then provoked the rival on social media with references to Vasco's 15-year title drought, the 'bacalhau' nickname, and Easter. Posts included messages like 'Calma, Família! Essa dança aqui precisa de cadência' and 'FELIZ PÁSCOA, FAMÍLIA BOTAFOGO!', along with nods to Vasco's historical edge in the fixture. The taunts followed Botafogo's comeback win, with the club declaring 'virou lição' (lesson learned).

item.title: PGR recorre de decisão de Zanin que suspendeu efeitos de condenação de Garotinho e liberou candidatura
item.description: The Attorney General's Office (PGR) appealed to the STF against Minister Cristiano Zanin's decision to suspend the effects of Anthony Garotinho's conviction in the 'Chequinho' operation, allowing his candidacy. The PGR argues that the defense's request for reconsideration was already unanimously denied by the STF's Second Chamber, and re-presenting it before the First Chamber violates res judicata and procedural good faith, while defending the validity of the digital evidence obtained in the investigation.

item.title: Ala minoritária da PT aciona direção nacional contra filiação de Kátia Abreu
item.description: A minoritarian left-wing faction of the Workers' Party (PT) in Tocantins has requested the national leadership to invalidate the affiliation of former minister and senator Kátia Abreu, arguing her political record contradicts the party's statutes and represents agribusiness and landlord interests. Abreu officially joined the PT in Tocantins on Saturday, with reported encouragement from President Lula, who allegedly reinforced the invitation. The national leadership is expected to uphold her affiliation despite the internal opposition.

item.title: Após anunciar que terminaria o mandato, Wilson Lima renuncia ao governo do Amazonas
item.description: Amazonas Governor Wilson Lima (União) resigned on the deadline for electoral incompatibility, enabling him to run for Senate in 2026, despite previously stating he would serve out his term. His resignation, alongside Vice Governor Tadeu Souza (Republicanos), clears the way for a Senate race against Eduardo Braga (MDB) and Alberto Neto (PL). With both governor and lieutenant governor vacated, Assembly President Roberto Cidade (União Brasil) will assume interim control of the state.

item.title: O vilarejo alagoano que ganhou o apelido de Caribe Brasileiro tem 6 km de praia, águas cristalinas e um mar que parece pintado de azul-turquesa
item.description: Maragogi, in Alagoas, earned the nickname 'Brazilian Caribbean' for its 6 km of beach, crystal-clear waters, and coral reefs that form turquoise natural pools at low tide. Located 130 km from Maceió, the destination saw growing numbers of foreign tourists in 2026, driven by its preserved natural beauty and expanding tourism infrastructure. The northern Alagoan coast is experiencing a unique moment of international visibility, positioning itself as a high-end alternative to Caribbean destinations without leaving Brazil.

item.title: Janela partidária provoca reconfiguração na Câmara e amplia força do PL
item.description: The 2026 party window led to over 120 deputies changing parties, making the PL the largest bloc in the Chamber with 101 deputies, surpassing the PT, which dropped to 66. União Brasil and PDT suffered the biggest losses, losing 15 and 10 seats respectively, while PP, Podemos, PSDB, and PSB gained ground. The shifts were driven by regional and electoral strategies aimed at competitiveness in the October elections.

item.title: Pesquisas eleitorais: após novo Paraná Pesquisas, como fica disputa Flávio x Lula
item.description: The latest Paraná Pesquisas survey shows the presidential race between Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and Flávio Bolsonaro is in a technical tie in the first round, with Lula at 41.3% and Flávio at 37.8%, within the 2.2-point margin of error. In the second round, Flávio leads narrowly at 45.2% versus Lula’s 44.1%, also within the margin of error, indicating an extremely close contest. Additionally, 53.3% of respondents believe Lula does not deserve re-election, highlighting declining approval for the incumbent president.

item.title: Em conversa com Lula, Davi Alcolumbre lava as mãos sobre indicação de Messias ao STF
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre told Lula he cannot assist in getting Jorge Messias appointed to the STF, but will not obstruct the process. Messias has already spoken with nearly all senators, yet requires approval from the Constitution and Justice Committee and the full Senate. Alcolumbre has his own candidate for the vacancy: Senator Rodrigo Pacheco.

item.title: Lula deseja Feliz Páscoa nas redes sociais. Veja mensagem
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) posted a message on social media on Sunday, April 5, 2026, wishing Brazilians a happy Easter. In the post, he emphasized the holiday as a time of renewal and family unity, accompanied by photos featuring First Lady Janja and several children.

item.title: Brasil está atrasado na automação do IR frente a países emergentes
item.description: Brazil lags behind in income tax automation, with only 60% of taxpayers using pre-filled returns, while countries like Chile, Mexico, and South Africa have fully automated systems. Finance Minister Dario Durigan proposed a new system that would eliminate manual filing, requiring only validation of pre-collected data from the Federal Revenue. The proposal aims to improve efficiency, reduce errors and bureaucracy, aligning Brazil with G20 and OECD standards.

item.title: A reação de Flávio Bolsonaro à troca de farpas entre Eduardo e Nikolas
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro called for unity among Brazil's right wing after a public exchange of accusations between Eduardo Bolsonaro and Nikolas Ferreira, criticizing internal conflict while the country faces challenges. Eduardo accused Nikolas of disrespect and supporting profiles that wish death upon Jair Bolsonaro, following Nikolas's 'kkk' reaction and sharing of a Lula post about Pix. Nikolas only responded by agreeing with Flávio's call for unity, stating the right could return to power if each member fulfills their role.

item.title: Os rostos desconhecidos de um governo sem marca definida, o de Lula
item.description: Lula's government will have its ministry formed by December with largely unknown figures, as the main figures of his team are leaving to run in the 2026 elections. The article highlights the absence of visible leadership in the federal executive during this period, focusing on the imminent political transition. The content is exclusive to subscribers and requires registration for full access.

item.title: Em primeiros vídeos como pré-candidato, Haddad destaca investimentos federais em Rodoanel, metrô e outras obras de SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad, the PT’s pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo, launches his first televised advertisements highlighting federal investments in major projects such as the northern Rodoanel, the Intercity Train, Metro expansion, and housing programs, without directly naming Governor Tarcísio de Freitas. In the videos, Haddad claims the state fails to disclose the billions invested by the Lula administration, while Lula appears in one via phone call, stating 'São Paulo deserves much more'. The debate over credit for major infrastructure projects has become a central theme in the campaign, marked by public exchanges between the two administrations.

item.title: Banco do Brasil cobra taxas maiores que grandes bancos privados em várias modalidades de financiamento
item.description: Banco do Brasil charges higher interest rates than major private banks across several financing modalities, worsening household debt in Brazil. This disparity has been cited as a factor contributing to declining popularity of the Lula government, which is seeking urgent solutions to the overindebtedness crisis. The full article is available only to subscribers.

item.title: Corinthians x Internacional pelo Brasileirão: Escalações e detalhes da partida
item.description: Corinthians faces Internacional in a Brazilian Série A 2026 match, under pressure after eight winless games and a recent 3-1 loss to Fluminense. Dorival Júnior's team sits 15th with ten points, while Internacional is 16th with nine, both fighting to avoid relegation.

item.title: Tarcísio não é invencível e nós podemos derrotá-lo, diz Dirceu
item.description: Former Minister José Dirceu (PT) stated that Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans) is not invincible and that the PT can defeat him in the 2026 São Paulo state elections. He linked the political struggle to the historical trajectory of Brazil's working class and emphasized the importance of regaining control of the country's largest electoral college, despite polls showing Tarcísio leading with 49.1% versus Haddad's 42.6%.

item.title: 10 governadores e 10 prefeitos de capitais renunciam para serem candidatos na eleição
item.description: Ten governors and ten state capital mayors resigned their offices to run in the 2026 elections, complying with Brazil’s incompatibility law. Among the governors, Romeu Zema and Ronaldo Caiado are pre-candidates for president, while the other eight aim for Senate seats. The rule aims to prevent the use of public resources for campaigning, with candidacies to be officially registered in August.

item.title: Perfil do futuro corregedor do TSE reforça a expectativa de tom moderado na Corte
item.description: The future corregedor-geral of the TSE, Minister Ricardo Villas Bôas Cueva from the STJ, will assume the role in September with a discreet profile and expertise in artificial intelligence. His leadership is expected to bring a more moderate tone to the Court, contrasting with the punitive rigor of 2022, despite anticipations of a polarized election between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Delação de Vorcaro pode ser homologada na reta final das eleições
item.description: The 90-day deadline for the homologation of Daniel Vorcaro's plea deal by the STF has expired, according to sources involved in the agreement. The exclusive report suggests the final decision may occur in the final stretch of the 2026 elections. Full access requires a subscription via the provided link.

item.title: Eduardo Bolsonaro ataca Nikolas, e Flávio tenta conter danos: ‘Inimigo não está aqui’
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro accused Nikolas Ferreira of opportunism and of keeping Flávio Bolsonaro in a silence spiral, claiming Nikolas gives projection to figures who hate the Bolsonaro family. Flávio Bolsonaro attempted to de-escalate the conflict by sharing a pacifying video, stating the enemy is not within the group. Nikolas did not respond to the accusations.

item.title: Lei veta multa em contagem de tempo anterior a filiação ao INSS
item.description: Law 15.363/2026, signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on March 27, 2026, abolishes fines for rural workers who performed activities before mandatory INSS enrollment, allowing them to count that period toward retirement without paying retroactive contributions plus penalties. The measure primarily benefits those who worked until 1991, when they were optional RGPS contributors, and originated from PLS 793/2015 by Senator Paulo Paim (PT-RS), approved in the Social Affairs Commission in 2021 with a favorable report by Senator Flávio Arns (PSB-PR).

item.title: Flávio critica “picuinhas” após bate-boca de Eduardo com Nikolas
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), a pre-candidate for President, criticized the 'squabbles' among right-wing leaders following a clash between Eduardo Bolsonaro and Nikolas Ferreira. He called for rationality within the group and stated that the country needs to be 'rescued', expressing concern over internal divisions as Brazil faces challenges.

item.title: Sem Eduardo e Zambelli, PL tem nova aposta para puxar votos em SP
item.description: With Carla Zambelli and Eduardo Bolsonaro ineligible for the 2026 elections, the PL is betting on São Paulo city councilman Lucas Pavanatto as its main vote-getter for the Chamber of Deputies. Pavanatto, who received 161,300 votes in 2024 for councilman, is projected to win around 800,000 votes in 2026, as four of the PL’s top five vote-getters in São Paulo in 2022 have left the party or are barred from running.

item.title: Lula exagera ao comparar reforma agrária entre governos
item.description: Lula claims the PT leads Brazil's agrarian reform, but Incra data shows land expropriation—the main tool of reform—has weakened under PT governments, with zero hectares expropriated in the first two years of his third term. Since FHC, expropriation dropped from over 10 million hectares to zero in 2022, resuming only in 2025 with 13,308 hectares. The Lula government has prioritized land purchase, donation, and regularization, acquiring 577,600 hectares through these means between 2023 and 2026.

item.title: Bolsonaristas descobrem dívidas de até R$ 15,5 milhões em multas por bloqueio de rodovias em 2022
item.description: Hundreds of Bolsonaro supporters have recently discovered they owe up to R$15.5 million in fines for illegally blocking highways in 2022 after contesting Lula’s election victory. The penalties stem from actions deemed antidemocratic by Brazil’s Supreme Court. The full article is available only to subscribers.

item.title: Com EUA de olho, governo avança em conselho de minerais críticos
item.description: The Lula government is advancing the creation of a strategic council on critical minerals linked to the Presidency, aimed at coordinating Brazil’s position in international negotiations over lithium, copper, nickel, manganese, and graphite. The body, still under discussion regarding its placement (either in the Civil House or the Ministry of Mines and Energy), seeks to centralize decisions and act as a bridge with the private sector, modeled after the CNPE. The final decision rests with the president, following meetings with ministers and advisors including Alexandre Silveira, Dario Durigan, and Celso Amorim.

item.title: “Kkk” e “deboche”: entenda a briga entre Nikolas e Eduardo
item.description: A public confrontation between Eduardo Bolsonaro and Nikolas Ferreira, both members of the PL party, was triggered by disagreements over support for Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-candidacy and differing interpretations of social media posts. The exchange, including Nikolas's use of 'kkk' and Eduardo's criticism of a 'mocking smile,' reflected existing tensions within the Bolsonarist base, culminating in Flávio Bolsonaro's appeal for unity and rationality among right-wing leaders.

item.title: Flávio testa slogan criado a partir de piada de comediante não bolsonarista
item.description: Comedian Murilo Couto created the joke 'meu amigo Flávio' to mock Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, but the right-wing has reappropriated the phrase and may turn it into a campaign slogan for Flávio Bolsonaro’s 2026 presidential bid under the PL party.

item.title: Tal pai (Jair), tal filho (Flávio Bolsonaro)
item.description: The text criticizes the amateurism and subservience of Jair Bolsonaro to the United States, particularly in his 2022 meeting with Biden, where he allegedly asked for help to defeat Lula. Flávio Bolsonaro, speaking at CPAC in Texas, repeats the same stance, presenting himself as 'Bolsonaro 2.0' and promising to be a 'reliable partner' for Trump, claiming Brazil will be the battlefield against China in critical minerals and asserting that elections will only be free and fair if he wins.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro puxa deputados para o PL e partido tem o maior ganho da janela partidária na Câmara
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) drove the PL’s largest gain in the partisan window, adding 12 federal deputies after 20 new affiliations and 8 departures, reaching 97 members in the Chamber. The PSD gained 7 deputies, while União Brasil lost 17, tying with PSD at 47. The shifts reflect a right- and center-right realignment aimed at boosting re-election prospects through alignment with Bolsonaro’s pre-candidacy and improved campaign access.

item.title: Lula planeja "bater bumbo" sobre um tema na próxima viagem à Europa
item.description: President Lula plans to highlight the Mercosul-European Union trade agreement during his upcoming trips to Spain and Germany in April, including participation at the Hannover Messe. The government views the treaty as a key achievement in its international agenda and aims to increase its visibility, with the agreement set to enter into provisional force on May 1st.

item.title: Lula pensa em criar a Terrabrás, mais um mito do Eldorado e outra ilusão econômica
item.description: Lula is considering the creation of Terrabrás, a state-owned enterprise aimed at strengthening Brazil's control over strategic resources such as fuels, fertilizers, and critical minerals to enhance its bargaining power in international agreements. The initiative is portrayed as a new Eldorado myth and an economic illusion, suggesting it may not bring direct wealth but could improve Brazil's position in global political-economic arrangements.

item.title: STF analisa na quarta ações que discutem a eleição para o governo do Rio; entenda o que está em jogo
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court will analyze on Wednesday (8) two actions regarding the Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial election, deciding whether it will be direct or indirect and validating parts of the state electoral law. The state has lacked an elected governor since Cláudio Castro's resignation in March 2025, with governance currently under the intervenorship of TJ-RJ President Ricardo Couto. The case involves figures such as Rodrigo Bacellar, who was removed and arrested, and will determine how the interim governor is selected until 2027.

item.title: Entrada de Caiado na corrida presidencial atrai o agronegócio e esfria adesão a Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The entry of former Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) into the 2026 presidential race is expected to split agribusiness support, which had been leaning toward Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ). Caiado’s long-standing ties to the agricultural sector and his record of pro-agro policies — evidenced by a 23% increase in Goiás’ grain exports in 2025 — may hinder Bolsonaro’s hopes for broad early-campaign backing.

item.title: Lula e a comunicação
item.description: The article claims that the Lula 3.0 government has a communication problem rooted not in strategy but in the president himself, whose repetitive and archaic agenda dominates institutional spaces. It argues that communication fails because Lula, as the central figure, is outdated and disconnected from contemporary demands. The content is subscriber-only, requiring registration for full access.
